title: vepris elliotii
intro: Vepris elliotii, also known as the Wild Privet or Elliot's Vepris, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family, commonly found in tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Vepris elliotii include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as an anthelmintic to expel worms from the body. Herbal preparations of Vepris elliotii can be made by infusing the leaves or bark in water, which can be used to treat various health ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
